mechanism_narrative: A rising trend in real retail sales (RSAFS) signals strengthening consumer demand that translates into higher corporate revenues and earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC for RSAFS changes remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of top-quartile RSAFS growth months.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points over three months signals a rapidly tightening labor market that boosts aggregate income growth and consumer confidence, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Walk-forward directional accuracy of the UNRATE momentum signal falls below 53% over any 2-year rolling window, or mean 5-day forward return during triggered periods is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Jointly accelerating consumer prices (CPIAUCSL) and a near-zero federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) produce deeply negative real interest rates that force investors out of cash and into equities, lifting S&P 500 valuations over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction term between CPIAUCSL changes and FEDFUNDS levels shows a Rank IC below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or the joint signal underperforms the sum of independent single-series signals by more than 50% in directional accuracy.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recoveries, month-over-month increases in industrial production (INDPRO) signal supply-side normalization and capacity utilization gains that support corporate profit margins, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: During the 24 months following the recession trough, INDPRO growth is associated with negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of months, or the regime-conditional Sharpe ratio is indistinguishable from zero (t-stat < 1.0).

mechanism_narrative: Increases in new private housing starts (HOUST) reflect durable demand strength in interest-rate-sensitive sectors and generate positive spillovers to construction, materials, and household durable consumption, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: HOUST changes exhibit a Rank IC below 0.02 over any 5-year rolling window, or positive 60-day returns occur in less than 52% of top-quartile HOUST growth months.

mechanism_narrative: When the effective federal funds rate rises above 0.25 percent, the exit from the zero lower bound increases the discount rate on future corporate cash flows and tightens financial conditions, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 0.25%, or the mean forward return during those periods is not statistically negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) and declines in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) confirm a broad-based labor-market recovery rather than one driven by labor-force exits, validating aggregate income expansion and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to achieve directional accuracy above 53% over any 3-year rolling window, or a partial F-test shows the interaction adds no marginal information (p > 0.10) when PAYEMS and UNRATE are already included.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP (GDPC1) exceeding 2.5 percent annualized signals aggregate demand outperformance that reduces near-term recession probability and supports earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are negative in more than 50% of quarters when GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.5% annualized, or the mean excess return during triggered quarters is not positive (t-test p > 0.05).
